name: shared-core
description: Language-agnostic orchestration, meta-skills, governance. Any language adapter depends on this.

aspirational_guardrails.G81: Baselines-not-lowered enforcement. Design rule decided in v0.2.0; script not yet authored.
aspirational_guardrails.G83: Per-run decision-log entry caps. Caps documented in CLAUDE.md rule 11; script not yet authored.
aspirational_guardrails.G84: Output-shape hash compensating baseline. Decided in CLAUDE.md rule 28 v0.3.0; script not yet authored.

baselines.scope_note: v0.4.0 ships with files moved to baselines/shared/ per Decision D1=B. Per-agent quality + per-skill stability are intrinsically language-neutral; one shared partition. All consumers (baseline-manager, metrics-collector, learning-engine, G86) updated to read the partitioned paths. Decision D2 (resolved 2026-04-27 post-R2 spike): Lenient default — one shared score. Progressive fallback if Phase B Python pilot shows ≥3pp quality_score divergence on a specific debt-bearer (that agent flips to per-language partition until D6=Split rewrite lands in its conventions.yaml). See docs/LANGUAGE-AGNOSTIC-DECISIONS.md + docs/R2-DEBT-REWRITE-FEASIBILITY.md.

skills.name: tdd-patterns
skills.reason: examples use Go `*_test.go` and `testing.T`; TDD cycle itself is language-neutral

skills.name: idempotent-retry-safety
skills.reason: code snippets use Go context + errgroup; retry classification generalizes

generalization_debt.resolution_policy: v0.5.0 Step 13 resolved all 4 debt-bearer AGENTS via D6=Split: rule body stays in shared-core/<agent>.md (language-neutral statements with [rule-key:<key>] tags), per-language flavor lives in `.claude/package-manifests/<lang>/conventions.yaml` keyed by agent + rule-key. Resolved agents (no longer debt): sdk-design-devil, sdk-overengineering-critic, sdk-semver-devil, sdk-security-devil. The 3 remaining SKILL debt items follow the same Split pattern in a future pass — universal rule body in shared-core skill file, language examples in <lang>/conventions.yaml under a `skills:` top-level key (schema extends conventions.yaml v1.0).

v0.5.0_step13.loader_protocol: Each agent's Startup Protocol reads runs/<run-id>/context/active-packages.json:target_language and loads .claude/package-manifests/<lang>/conventions.yaml as LANG_CONVENTIONS. Each rule in the agent body has a [rule-key:<key>] tag matching LANG_CONVENTIONS.agents.<agent-name>.rules.<key>.

notes.pass_scope: v0.5.0 manifest-schema lock. Adds `waves` (per-wave agent contributions) + `tier_critical` (per-phase tier-gating) so phase leads can dispatch from manifest data instead of hardcoded prompt lists. Phase lead refactor (Step 3 of structure-finalization plan) consumes these fields.
notes.waves_field: Each entry maps wave-id → list of THIS package's agents that fire in that wave. Phase lead unions across active packages: ACTIVE_AGENTS_FOR_WAVE = ⋃ (pkg.waves[wave] for pkg in active-packages). Mode-specific waves use suffix `_mode_bc` (e.g. D3_devils_mode_bc) — lead conditionally adds when mode∈{B,C}. Wave-id naming: <phase-letter><number>_<short-purpose> (I=intake, D=design, M=impl, T=testing, F=feedback).
notes.tier_critical_field: Per-phase, per-tier list of THIS package's agents the phase lead REQUIRES in active-packages union; halt with BLOCKER if any missing. Phase lead unions per (phase, tier) across active packages.
notes.contains_drift_gates: G06, G90 (strict), G116 ship in shared-core because drift-prevention is language-neutral and is load-bearing for every run. Removing them from shared-core would let intake proceed on a drifted repo.
notes.contains_meta_baselines: G81 (baseline-advance), G83 (determinism), G84 (output-shape hash), G85 (learning-notifications), G86 (quality regression) are cross-run observability gates; language-neutral.
notes.G04_mcp_health: WARN-only by design (rule 31). Shared-core because MCP fallback policy applies to any language.
